design_philosophy.core_loop: Move right through side-scrolling levels, jump over hazards and enemies, reach flagpole at end of stage. Collect power-ups to gain temporary advantages (size increase, fire attack, invincibility). Master enemy behavior patterns and level layout.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Horizontal momentum vs. vertical precision - player must maintain forward progress while executing precise platforming. Time pressure (level timer) creates secondary tension.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue Princess Toadstool by traversing a colorful, dangerous kingdom. Transformation fantasy (growing larger via mushroom). Mastery fantasy through learning enemy patterns and hidden secrets.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: World 1-1 designed as intentional tutorial that teaches mechanics without explicit instruction. Precise controls are centerpiece - every level teaches a principle progressively. Hidden secrets and multiple paths reward exploration. Music integrated as design element, not afterthought. Enemies have distinct behavioral patterns requiring different approaches rather than generic collision damage.

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with set number of lives. Extra lives earned via: green 1-Up mushrooms in hidden bricks, collecting 100 coins, defeating multiple enemies consecutively with Koopa shell, bouncing on enemies without touching ground. Collecting all coins in world's third level spawns hidden life bricks.
difficulty.continues_system: Game over allows respawn from first level of world where player died by holding A button. No true continues system; progression resets to world checkpoint.
difficulty.checkpointing: World-based checkpoint system. Dying returns player to start of current world, not current level. Encourages replay of familiar levels.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-ups provide player choice in risk/reward (take mushroom for damage resistance vs. remain small for maneuverability). Secret item discovery and Koopa shell mechanics reward skilled play. Hidden brick locations and warping paths offer optional difficulty modulation.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups collected during levels: Super Mushroom (size increase, brick-breaking), Fire Flower (projectile attack), Starman (temporary invincibility). No persistent upgrades across levels.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight worlds unlock sequentially. Warping available by collecting all coins in world's third level, allowing sequence skip. World structure gates progression linearly.
replayability_hooks: Hidden bricks and secret items encourage re-exploration of levels
Coin collection thresholds unlock warp zones
Multiple paths through levels (ground vs. elevated routes)
Enemy shell mechanics reward skillful enemy manipulation
Successive enemy bouncing for bonus lives incentivizes chaining attacks
Multiplayer mode with alternating player turns creates competitive replay value

world_building_techniques: Thematic level progression (grassland → underground → water → castle)
Enemy and hazard variety suggests hostile kingdom ecosystem
Princess rescue framing provides single narrative goal
Bowser presence in final castle (implicit from series knowledge)
Mushroom Kingdom setting implied through visual design and item names

danger_signals: Enemy types escalate in difficulty (Goombas → Koopa Troopas → Lakitus)
environmental_storytelling: Progression from natural (grassland) to industrial (underground pipes) to aquatic (water levels) to castle (final domain)
Question-mark brick mysteries encourage curiosity and reward exploration
Hidden secret areas suggest kingdom complexity beyond visible paths
Mushroom Kingdom flora and fauna reflect colorful, stylized world distinct from realistic settings

sources_used: wikipedia - Super Mario Bros. article (gameplay section, development notes, design philosophy)
